en.extract:
The chestnut-bellied nuthatch (Sitta cinnamoventris) is a species of bird belonging to the family Sittidae.
It is found in the Indian subcontinent occurring in the countries of India, Tibet, Bangladesh, Bhutan, and Nepal.
It is found in subtropical or tropical forest, that are dry or moist forests, and in montane and lowland forests.
This species is very similar to the Burmese nuthatch but it has a heavier bill, the crown and mantle are of the same shade. The wing and tail markings show contrasting markings; silvery-edge to primaries, blackish inner webs to tertials and tail with large white spots in the tail. Rasmussen and Anderton (2005) established the chestnut-bellied nuthatch is different from the Indian nuthatch and the Burmese nuthatch.  White on ear coverts does not extend into chin unlike in the Indian nuthatch. Race almorae of Nepal and NW Himalayas has paler underparts; race koelzi of the eastern Himalayas has the female darker than in other races. Resident from Murree Hills to the Uttaranchal foothills extending to the Assam Valley, Arunachal Pradesh into the Lushai Hills.


References

The '''chestnut-bellied nuthatch''' ('''''Sitta cinnamoventris''''') is a species of bird belonging to the family [[Sittidae]].

It is found in the [[Indian subcontinent]] occurring in the countries of [[India]], [[Tibet]], [[Bangladesh]],<ref>{{Cite journal|url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/chbnut3/cur/introduction|title=Chestnut-bellied Nuthatch (Sitta cinnamoventris)|first=Simon|last=Harrap|date=March 4, 2020|journal=Birds of the World|via=birdsoftheworld.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://datazone.birdlife.org/species/factsheet/chestnut-bellied-nuthatch-sitta-cinnamoventris/details |title=Chestnut-bellied Nuthatch Sitta cinnamoventris |website=datazone.birdlife.org}}</ref> [[Bhutan]], and [[Nepal]].

It is found in subtropical or tropical forest, that are dry or moist forests, and in montane and lowland forests.

This species is very similar to the [[Burmese nuthatch]] but it has a heavier bill, the crown and mantle are of the same shade. The wing and tail markings show contrasting markings; silvery-edge to primaries, blackish inner webs to tertials and tail with large white spots in the tail. Rasmussen and Anderton (2005)<ref name=pcr>Rasmussen, P. C. & J. Anderton (2005) The Birds of South Asia:The Ripley Guide. Smithsonian Institution and Lynx Edicions.</ref> established the chestnut-bellied nuthatch is different from the [[Indian nuthatch]] and the [[Burmese nuthatch]].  White on ear coverts does not extend into chin unlike in the Indian nuthatch. Race ''almorae'' of Nepal and NW Himalayas has paler underparts; race ''koelzi'' of the eastern Himalayas has the female darker than in other races. Resident from Murree Hills to the Uttaranchal foothills extending to the Assam Valley, Arunachal Pradesh into the Lushai Hills.

zh.extract:
栗腹䴓（台湾作東方栗腹鳾）（学名：Sitta cinnamoventris），是雀形目䴓科的一种鸟类。该物种的模式产地在印度大吉岭。


特征
栗腹䴓体重约19.62克，翼长约80.6毫米，嘴峰长约19.7毫米，喙宽度约4.3毫米，喙厚度约4.2毫米，跗蹠长约18.6毫米，尾长约43.6毫米。栗腹䴓在其分布区内为留鸟，其栖息在密集的高大树木组成的森林中，食性为肉食性，主要食物来源是陆生无脊椎动物。


亚种
S. c. almorae：分布于喜马拉雅山西麓（巴基斯坦至尼泊尔东部）。
S. c. koelzi：分布于阿萨姆邦东部至阿萨姆邦和邻近的缅甸西北部。
S. c. tonkinensis：分布于滇南至泰国北部、越南北部、老挝北部和中部。
S. c. cinnamoventris：分布于喜马拉雅山脉东部（尼泊尔东部至云南西北部和西藏东南部）。
